title: October 2024 Monthly Manager’s Report
summary: October 8, 2024 SUMMARY Good afternoon! I hope you are enjoying the vibrant colors of fall in Southcentral Alaska as much as I am. However, the transition to winter is just around the corner. This month, our team has been busy preparing for the changing seasons, ensuring our operations are ready to meet the challenges ahead. As is often the case, the combination of hunting season and travel for various affiliate meetings has made the past month fly by. I had the opportunity to attend the Alaska Power Association (APA) Annual Meeting in Fairbanks, the National Rural Electric Cooperative Association and Cooperative Finance Corporation Region 7 and 9 Meeting in Sacramento, California, and a CFC Board Meeting in Dulles, Virginia. Each of these experiences was invaluable. Looking ahead, we have our community meetings scheduled for later this month, providing excellent opportunities to connect with our members and address their needs as we...

title: Enhancing Road Safety: Report Your Concerns with the State of Alaska’s New Reporting Tool
summary: The State of Alaska has created an online tool for the public to use to report issues and safety concerns with roadways maintained by the State. title: Clean Energy Funding for Alaskan Homes & Businesses
summary: Clean Energy Funding for Alaskan Homes & Businesses An unprecedented amount of funding for renewable energy and energy efficiency (together, “clean energy”) projects is currently being made available for Alaskans. With such a wealth of opportunity available to us, determining which opportunities to pursue and how to do so can seem daunting. That is why REAP hosted a webinar featuring subject matter experts to present key information and answer audience questions on clean energy funding for Alaskan homes and businesses for the first installment of our 2024 Fall Energy Speaker Series! The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) introduced and expanded tax credits for clean energy technologies including game-changing new provisions that enable tax-exempt and governmental entities—such as states, local governments, Tribes, territories, and nonprofits—to take an active role in building the clean energy economy. The IRA also invested billions of dollars in rebate programs aimed at providing low- and m...
